raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, I purchased three XXXX tickets through Flights Channel using my American Express card. I used the outbound flights in XX/XX/year>, but my return flights in XX/XX/year> were lost after XXXX  website showed a message : Your seat has been changedrequest a refund. I never requested a cancellation. 

XXXX  policy states refunds for third-party bookings must go through the agency, not XXXX. Yet they processed a small refund of {$140.00} and reported to Amex that I requested it. Their own record ( XX/XX/year>, ref. XXXX ) lists the reason as I was downgraded, confirming it was not voluntary. 

Amex first closed my disputes without a written decision, then closed them again saying no response received, even though I had uploaded full documentation ( XXXX refund record, policy, screenshots, and receipts ). On XX/XX/year> Amex told me by phone they never received or reviewed my evidence before closing. This violates their duty to conduct a reasonable investigation under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( Reg. Z 1026.13 ).
